concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The 'TABLE OF CONTENTS' section on page 2 is severely truncated. Due to an extraction artifact (represented by a long string of zeros), the output stops after listing section 3.1 and completely omits the remainder of the table of contents (sections 3.1.1 through 6.0).

Multiple functional hyperlinks in the source PDF were extracted as plain text without any hyperlink properties (`href` or markdown syntax). These missing links include several descriptive links in the text as well as the entirety of the reference links in a table.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1. 'Risk Evaluation Resources' (Page 5)
2. 'USEPA Regional Screening Level (RSL) Tables' (Page 5)
3. 'Technical Guidance for Risk-Based Environmental Remediation of Sites' (Page 5)
4. 'Risk-Based Remediation Website' (Page 8)
5. Clickable entries in the 'List of Mutagenic Contaminants' table such as 'IRIS', 'Supplemental Guidance', etc. (Page 24)
concerns.output_quote: The text for these links is extracted as plain text (e.g., `"text": "Risk Evaluation Resources"`), completely lacking an associated URL or anchor formatting.

concerns.analysis: There are misplaced images disrupting text flow at page boundaries. The house icons from the diagram on page 8 were extracted separately and incorrectly placed in the middle of a continuous sentence that spans the page break between pages 8 and 9. This misplaces the images relative to their intended context and breaks the text flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 8: "Due to the complexity of the Risk Calculator, a few minutes may be needed to complete the" followed on Page 9 by "download. Also, you may receive a security message..."
concerns.output_quote:
`

. Due to the complexity of the Risk Calculator, a few minutes may be needed to complete the

download. Also, you may receive a security message...`

concerns.description: Missing table(s): source PDF has tables not found in output on p6(source:1,output:0), p8(source:2,output:1), p25(source:1,output:0)
concerns.analysis: source_tables=7, output_tables=5. Pages with missing tables: p6(source:1,output:0), p8(source:2,output:1), p25(source:1,output:0)

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (11 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=11. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (69): Risk Calculator User Guide; Risk Calculator User Guide; February 2024
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=69, pages_affected=31/33. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description:
Text split by page break (1 instance(s)): p4→p5: “… Center*
*Raleigh, North Carolina 27699*” → “page on the NCDEQ Risk-Based Remediation…”
concerns.analysis: split_paragraph_count=1. Paragraphs that span page breaks should be merged into a single continuous element. Split sentences disrupt reading order for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "ASTM"; 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1, no_th_tables=1. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 34 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (31 truncated mid-sentence, 3 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "...y. The design features a blue outline map of North"; p6: "...nmental contaminants from initial release to human"; p8: "... and a black house icon on the right, separated by"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=34 (truncated=31, placeholder=3, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document uses underlined text for emphasis in several places (which is distinct from hyperlinks). This underline emphasis is completely lost in the extracted JSON output. Examples include the words 'Current' and 'future' on page 7, 'each detected contaminant' on page 20, and 'individual' and 'Cumulative' on page 22.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Current risk to identified receptors should be evaluated as well as future risk to potential future land-use scenarios...
concerns.output_quote: Current risk to identified receptors should be evaluated as well as future risk to potential future land-use scenarios...

concerns.description: Table row count mismatch: p8(source:2rows,output:13rows), p24(source:14rows,output:27rows)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have >20% row count difference (delta >=3). Pages: p8(source:2rows,output:13rows), p24(source:14rows,output:27rows)
Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p24(source:9cols,output:3cols)

concerns.description: Degenerate images detected: 4 images with extreme aspect ratio or tiny dimensions
Degraded image quality: 1 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=1. Examples: p19: "A user interface displaying a menu titled "DATA IN". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "Chemical with a Henry's Law constant less than or ". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.
